blocks.text: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth <muted>(of all living and non-living things)</muted> glorifies Allāh <muted>(with His perfection and praise, in acknowledgement of His Lordship)</muted>, and He is the All-Mighty <muted>(in just recompense)</muted>, the All-Wise <muted>(in the governance of His creation)</muted>.
O you who believe, why do you say that which you do not do <muted>(when it is enjoined upon you)</muted>?
It is greatly hated to Allāh that you say that which you do not do.
Indeed, Allāh loves those who fight in His cause in a row, as if they are a compact structure <muted>(firmly fixed without gaps, unified and steadfast in their intent and goal)</muted>.

And <muted>(mention, O Muhammad, to those who say that which they do not do)</muted> when Moses said to his people: “O my people, why do you harm me <muted>(with your disobedience and opposition to me)</muted> while you certainly know that I am Allāh’s messenger to you?”

So when they deviated <muted>(by disobeying the commanded of Moses to fight in Allāh’s cause against the Amalekites in the Holy Land for the establishment of tawhīd)</muted>, Allāh caused their hearts to deviate <muted>(from being receptive to truth)</muted>, and Allāh does not guide the defiantly sinful people.

And <muted>(mention, O Muhammad, to those who say that which they do not do)</muted> when Jesus, son of Mary, said: “O Children of Israel, I am Allāh’s messenger to you, confirming what came before me of the Torah, and giving good tidings of a messenger to come after me whose name is Ahmad <muted>(the most praising of His Lord’s perfection and glory and the most praised by men for his virtues)</muted>.”

But when he <muted>(the prophesied messenger, Muhammad)</muted> came to them <muted>(the Israelites in and around Madīnah)</muted> with clear evidences <muted>(that he is the prophet spoken of by Jesus, that he confirms the Torah and guides them away from their errors)</muted>, they said: “This is plain magic.”

And who is more unjust than the one who fabricates a lie against Allāh <muted>(that His signs are magic, or that His prophet is a magician)</muted> while he is being invited to Islām <muted>(the religion of Abraham, Moses and Jesus, to worship only one Lord)</muted>. And Allāh does not guide the oppressive people <muted>(who knowingly do wrong)</muted>.

They desire to extinguish Allāh’s light <muted>(of truth and guidance)</muted> with their mouths <muted>(by their fabrications and lies)</muted>, but Allāh will perfect His light <muted>(through Muhammad’s victory and then the descent of Jesus)</muted>, even though the disbelievers <muted>(of all factions)</muted> detest it.

It is He Who sent His messenger with the guidance <muted>(of the Qur’ān)</muted> and the religion of truth <muted>(comprising tawhīd and just law)</muted> to <muted>(aid it and)</muted> make it manifest  over all other religion <muted>(through proofs and evidences and the descent of Jesus)</muted>, even though the polytheists detest it.
O you who believe, shall I guide you to a transaction that will save you from a painful punishment?

<muted>(It is that)</muted> you believe in Allāh and His messenger and strive hard in Allāh’s cause with your wealth and your lives <muted>(to make His word and that of His messengers uppermost)</muted>. That is best for you, if you were to know.

He will forgive you your sins and admit you to gardens beneath which rivers flow, and <muted>(to)</muted> pleasant dwellings in gardens of eternity. That is the great achievement <muted>(of success)</muted>.

And <muted>(you will receive)</muted> another <muted>(blessing in this life)</muted> that you love: aid from Allāh <muted>(against your enemies)</muted> and an imminent victory <muted>(through people entering Islām in droves, and the peaceful conquest of Mecca)</muted>. And give glad tidings to the believers <muted>(so that they may participate in this transaction)</muted>.

O you who believe, be helpers <muted>(in the cause)</muted> of Allāh <muted>(in this aforementioned transaction and respond)</muted> as when Jesus, son of Mary, said to the disciples: “Who are my helpers for <muted>(the cause of)</muted> Allāh?”, the disciples responded: “We are Allāh’s helpers <muted>(who will strive in His cause)</muted>.”

So a faction of the Children of Israel believed <muted>(that he is Allāh’s servant and messenger)</muted> and a faction disbelieved <muted>(by denying that he is Allāh’s Messenger, or saying he is Allāh or is Allāh’s son)</muted>,

so <muted>(with the sending of Muhammad)</muted> We supported those who believed <muted>(in the true call of Jesus)</muted> against their enemy <muted>(who had rejected Jesus, or persecuted his true followers, or altered his religion)</muted>, and they became uppermost.
